case_title: MARK THIERMAN et al VS. JOHN KELSON

docket_entries.description: REPLY IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER KELSON, JOHN
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-18-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOHN M. KELSON'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATOR CORRECTLY EXERCISED HER AUTHORITY UNDER LOPEZ V. MILLSAP (1992) 6 CAL.APP.4TH 1679, 1687 TO CORRECT A COMPUTATIONAL ERROR IN THE INITIAL AWARD. THE CORRECTED FINAL AWARD IS CONFIRMED. PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO COSTS, INTEREST AT 10% FROM OCTOBER 17, 2008 AND REASONABLE ATTORNEYS IN CONNECTION WITH THIS PROCEEDING. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-17-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS MARK THIERMAN AND H. TIM HOFFMAN'S Petition To Confirm/Vacate Arbitration Awards.
APPEARANCES: David Borgen, Esq. of Goldstein, Demchak, Baller, Borgen & Dardarian 510/763-9800 for moving parties MARK THIERMAN AND H. TIM HOFFMAN; Niall P. McCarthy, Esq. & Justin T. Berger, Esq. of Cotchette, Pitre & McCarthy 650/697-6000 for opposing party John M. Kelson.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR CORRECTLY EXERCISED HER AUTHORITY UNDER LOPEZ V. MILLSAP (1992) 6 CAL.APP.4TH 1679, 1687 TO CORRECT A COMPUTATIONAL ERROR IN THE INITIAL AWARD. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
